department_opportunity_map.opportunity_signal: Create earned and stakeholder storylines around the brand's most credible proof points, customer outcomes, and category momentum.
department_opportunity_map.rationale: PR & Comms can turn the strongest evidence in the report into newsworthy narratives and reputation-building proof.

department_opportunity_map.opportunity_signal: Build clearer buyer journeys, proof modules, editorial explainers, and conversion content from the report's messaging gaps.
department_opportunity_map.rationale: Content can make the proposition easier to understand, compare, and trust across owned surfaces.

department_opportunity_map.opportunity_signal: Translate the strongest positioning and proof themes into search journeys, CRM paths, paid tests, and conversion experiments.
department_opportunity_map.rationale: Digital Marketing can connect the sharper story to measurable demand, traffic, and lead quality.

department_opportunity_map.opportunity_signal: Clarify how the proposition should feel, sound, and flex across audiences, proof points, and commercial moments.
department_opportunity_map.rationale: Brands can help the strongest strategic idea become a recognisable, repeatable market-facing system.

department_opportunity_map.opportunity_signal: Turn the most compelling campaign territories into distinctive assets, motion, social cut-downs, and presentation-ready concepts.
department_opportunity_map.rationale: Creative Services can dramatise the opportunity once the central proof and activation ideas are agreed.

department_opportunity_map.opportunity_signal: Validate audience language, competitor framing, category white space, and the proof gaps most likely to affect buyer confidence.
department_opportunity_map.rationale: Insights & Intelligence can make the strategy sharper by grounding it in evidence, language, and market context.

verification_queries: Example Source A exact headline verification
Example Source B exact headline verification
discovery_sequence: Started with broad discovery queries across news, reputation, investor, customer, trade, and analyst contexts.
Scored all candidate stories before reduction using the weighted influence model.
Ran targeted verification only after scoring to confirm dates, source wording, URLs, and counter-evidence.
influence_ranking.ranking_method: Score candidate stories from 1 to 100 using source authority, buyer relevance, reputation risk or opportunity, evidence quality, novelty, and recency; order the final set by score descending.
search_queries: Example Brand customer trust reviews controversy 2026
Example Brand investors partnerships market performance 2026
Example Brand category growth analyst trade press 2026
influence_ranking.confidence_rationale: Example fixture confidence is sufficient because it demonstrates the required broad-first candidate pool, scoring model, source diversity, and verification sequence.
limitations: Example fixture uses representative placeholder sources rather than live coverage.

ideas.title: The Proof Foundry
ideas.illustration_prompt: Create a tall portrait campaign illustration for 'The Proof Foundry'. Use a risograph editorial broadside treatment. The core idea is: A proof-building campaign that turns the core commercial claim into a visible, repeatable evidence system.. Art direction: misregistered ink, radical print-shop texture, and manifesto-like proof stacking with visible production grit. Compose it to fill a narrow editorial column from top to bottom with a strong upper focal zone. Avoid generic flat corporate vector art. Make it visually distinct from the other campaign ideas and premium in finish. Hard constraints: no words, no letters, no numbers, no captions, no labels, no typography, no readable text, no UI copy, no signage. Do not show the target brand's logo, no logos, no target brand assets, no wordmark, no colour-coded brand assets, no product marks, no app screens, no packaging, and no recognisable owned brand identity. Use abstract or symbolic forms only for company/product references.
ideas.addresses: Replace with the weakness, objection, or opportunity this idea addresses.
ideas.concept: A proof-building campaign that turns the core commercial claim into a visible, repeatable evidence system.
ideas.activation: Replace with a one-sentence activation summary.

Compose it to fill a narrow editorial column from top to bottom with a strong upper focal zone. Avoid generic flat corporate vector art.
